detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has Netflixâs password-sharing ban impacted customer loyalty and 
brand image?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Demonstrates a strong understanding of ethical concepts, repeatedly linking trust, fairness and reputational risk to Netflixâs password-sharing ban
Makes clear, well-structured connections between ethics and organizational objectives in multiple sections (e.g., SWOT, evaluation)
weaknesses: Ethical considerations are not fully integrated into every analytical tool (e.g., Porter's Five Forces remains largely competitive)
Definitions and operational measures of key ethical constructs (customer loyalty, brand image) are not introduced or measured rigorously
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands were assessed at band 4. The student shows a clear grasp of ethics throughout but does not weave it seamlessly into every analytic section, warranting a final mark of 4/5.

strengths: Includes exactly five supporting documents, all properly labelled and referenced
Documents are relevant to loyalty, perception and financial impact and are cited within the text
weaknesses: Sources are largely journalistic or aggregated statistics rather than academic studies, limiting depth
Several data points (e.g., Q1 subscriber loss, $110 million increase) lack precise in-text citations to the supporting documents
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the numerical requirement (5 docs) and provides moderate depth and relevance, matching band 3. The lack of scholarly sources and citation gaps prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Appropriate use of SWOT and Porterâs Five Forces frameworks
Methodology section clearly outlines a mixed-methods approach and acknowledges limitations
weaknesses: Application of tools remains largely descriptive with few deeper strategic or data-driven insights
Selection of tools is adequate but narrow; other frameworks (e.g., loyalty metrics, Ansoff) are absent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student selects relevant tools and applies them adequately (band 3), the largely descriptive treatment and limited tool range justify reducing this to 2/4 to accommodate the overall mark constraint.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Presents a nuanced analysis contrasting short-term gains with long-term ethical and loyalty risks
Effectively segments and discusses primary survey results and financial data in the SWOT and evaluation
weaknesses: Analysis lacks detailed projections or multivariate techniques to isolate the password-ban effect
Primary sample is small and biased without dispersion measures for survey results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates mostly effective, sustained analysis with good integration of quantitative and qualitative data (band 4). The absence of advanced statistical treatment and broader sample justification precludes the top band.

strengths: Conclusions clearly answer the research question and are directionally consistent with the evidence
Acknowledges both negative short-term and potential positive long-term impacts
weaknesses: Conclusions rely on broad statements without quantified impact or alternative explanations
Evidence linkage is partial rather than comprehensive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band assessments were 2 for consistency and 3 for addressing the question. Given partial evidence linkage, a final mark of 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Logical table of contents, numbered headings and coherent flow throughout
Research question is clearly stated and guides the structure
weaknesses: Some TOC entries lack page numbers, hindering navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is fully appropriate (band 2) with only minor formatting issues, meriting the maximum 2/2.

strengths: All required elements (title page, word count, methodologies, appendices, works cited) are present
Charts and tables are embedded and referenced correctly
weaknesses: Lacks a concise subtitle outlining specific metrics or variables
Formatting of some figures could be improved for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all IB requirements (band 2) with only minor stylistic suggestions, warranting 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical table of contents, numbered headings and coherent flow are present throughout, meeting expectations for appropriate structure.

Documents include primary survey data, subscriber/revenue statistics and news/analyst reports.  They are relevant to loyalty, perception and financial impact, and are cited within the text.  Depth is moderateâsources are largely journalistic or compiled statistics rather than academic studiesâso they are âsufficientlyâ but not richly in-depth.
The IA includes title page details, word count, methodology, appendices and works-cited list.  Charts and tables are embedded and referenced, satisfying the required presentation elements.
Exactly five supporting documents are supplied, matching the recommended range.  They are properly referenced and labelled, so the numerical requirement is clearly met.

Both SWOT and Porterâs model are applied to the password-sharing decision; the student comments on rivalry, substitutes and ethical backlash.  Nevertheless, much of the application remains descriptive (e.g., listing each force) and connections to loyalty metrics are occasionally asserted rather than analysed.  Relevance is âadequateâ but not consistently sharp or data-driven.

The submission uses primary survey results, Netflix subscriber/revenue graphs and quotations from analyst reportsâcovering both quantitative and qualitative evidence.  Data are cited and displayed, but the primary survey is small and skewed, and not all figures are integrated into calculations.  Hence selection/use is âadequateâ rather than âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ.

Analysis moves beyond description by contrasting short-term revenue gains with long-term ethical and loyalty risks, and by segmenting perceptions by age.  These threads are drawn together in the evaluation.  However, the reasoning is sometimes general (e.g., âmay ensure sustainable growthâ) and lacks detailed financial or loyalty projections, so integration is not sustained at the highest level.

The research questionââto what extentâ¦ââis explicitly answered, with the student stating the impact is âconsiderably negativeâ¦in the short runâ but potentially beneficial long-term.  The response is clear and specific.

The IA employs a SWOT, Porterâs Five Forces and primary survey dataâan adequate suite for the research question.  Other potentially insightful tools (e.g., Ansoff, BCG, loyalty metrics) are absent, so selection is suitable but not especially wide or sophisticated.

The concluding statements broadly align with survey evidence and subscriber trends, recognising negative perception but positive revenue.  Yet limited reference is made to quantified impacts or alternative explanations, so consistency is partial rather than comprehensive.

Netflix is consistently at the centre of the discussion.  The policy, subscriber data and perception surveys are all directly tied back to the firmâs objectives, market rivalry and mission statement.  Links are made in the SWOT and in the final evaluation to how the decision affects Netflixâs dominance.  The connection is analysed in mostâbut not everyâsection; some tool write-ups remain generic. Therefore a strong, but not fully pervasive, organisational link is evident.

The student demonstrates a clear grasp of ethics, repeatedly linking the notion of trust, fairness and profit-orientation to the password-sharing ban. Ethical tension is explained when discussing the 2017 âLove is sharing a passwordâ tweet, the âbreach of trustâ felt by users and long-term reputational risks.  Ethical language resurfaces in the SWOT and evaluation, showing partial integration.  However, ethical considerations do not drive every part of the analysis (e.g., Porter's Five Forces is treated mainly competitively, not ethically).  Thus understanding is strong but not fully woven throughout.

annotations.comment: The table of contents presents a logical sequence and clear numbering, which aids navigation throughout the document.
Some entries in the table of contents lack page numbers (e.g., â2. Main Findingsâ), which may hinder the readerâs ability to locate sections quickly.
Ensure consistency in sub-section numbering and formatting (e.g., 1.2 Methodology vs 2.1 Survey), to maintain a professional structure.
The introduction provides solid background on Netflixâs history and competitive context, grounding the study in relevant industry developments.
The citation for the 200,000 subscriber loss in Q1 2022 is unclear; verify and reference the exact supporting document to bolster credibility.
The research question is clearly stated and focused, guiding the investigation effectively. This strong articulation sets a precise scope for analysis.
Ethics is identified as the key concept, but its definition and relevance could be more explicitly linked to loyalty and brand image in the introduction.
Consider adding a concise subtitle under the main heading to outline specific metrics or variables that will be measured for loyalty and brand image.
Define precisely how âcustomer loyaltyâ and âbrand imageâ will be measured in this study; include operational definitions in the introduction.
The methodology section clearly outlines the mixedâmethods approach and acknowledges its own limitations.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Relying on a 30-person sample of mainly classmates introduces significant bias; expand the sample to include more diverse age groups.
Include exact survey questions in an appendix or main text for clarity and reproducibility of primary data collection.
The reported mean of $6.13/10 lacks dispersion measures; include standard deviation or distribution charts to contextualize the average.
Report exact percentages and sample sizes alongside narrative statements on consideration of switching platforms to enhance credibility.
The use of Netflixâs quarterly financial data from its own reports strengthens the analysis with credible primary evidence.
Attributing the resurgence in subscriber growth solely to the password ban overlooks concurrent pricing and marketing changes.
Incorporate a multivariate analysis or simple regression to isolate the effect of the password ban from other growth drivers.
The analysis of competitive forces remains descriptive; deeper evaluation of strategic implications would strengthen the application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Integrate ethical considerations into each forceâe.g., analysing how fairness concerns affect buyer power and substitutes.
The SWOT table succinctly organizes strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats to inform the evaluation.
Some SWOT points (e.g., $110 million Q1 increase) are presented without clear source citations; link each to supporting documents.
Add parenthetical citations or footnote markers directly in the SWOT to tie each element back to exact data or document.
The presentation of mean scores and percentages helps illustrate user perceptions quantitatively.
Consider presenting a histogram or boxplot of responses in Figure 5 to show the spread and reinforce the statistical analysis.
The segmentation of responses by age group provides valuable insight into demographic differences in perception.
The claim that 15% were âheavily impactedâ is stated without supporting data points; quantify these categories explicitly.
Porterâs Five Forces is an appropriate framework for assessing industry pressures relevant to Netflixâs decision.
